description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the docker:stable-dind image with DOCKER_DRIVER set to overlay2. Define a single build stage with a job named build that runs a before_script to start the Docker daemon, wait for /var/run/docker.sock, log in to the GitLab registry using the CI_BUILD_TOKEN, and set NAME to $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:$(echo $CI_COMMIT_SHA|cut -c1-8). In the script it pulls the latest image and the commit‑specific image (ignoring failures), builds a new image with --build-arg BASIC_AUTH=latency-at-bot:$ACCESS_TOKEN and --cache-from $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest, tags it as $NAME and also as $CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE:latest, pushes both tags, and finally stops the Docker daemon. The pipeline has no caches, artifacts, or conditional rules.
